Reason and revelation · 12th – 14th c. CE

Jewish Tradition

Medieval Jewish philosophers take up the Aristotelian and Neoplatonic inheritance, often in active dialogue with Islamic thinkers, and ask how reason relates to a revealed tradition. Maimonides insists that the perfection of the intellect is the soul’s deepest purpose; Gersonides pushes Aristotelian naturalism as far as it will go.

1138 – 1204

Maimonides

Reason, prophecy, and the perfection of the intellect in the Guide of the Perplexed.

1288 – 1344

Gersonides (Ralbag)

An Aristotelian Jewish philosopher on intellect, immortality, and the limits of divine knowledge.

Digital Minds · A history of thinking about the mind, from antiquity to the present day.
A personal research project by Fabián. · About · Glossary
